database_info.name: Network Switch Selection Theory - Standards Based
database_info.purpose: Engineering design tool for network switch selection in surveillance systems

1_managed_vs_unmanaged.principle: Management level determines configuration capability and troubleshooting

comparison.best_for: Small systems <16 cameras, single subnet, no special requirements

comparison.features: VLAN, QoS, port mirroring, IGMP, bandwidth control
comparison.best_for: Medium systems, shared network, need traffic isolation

comparison.features: Full L2: VLAN, STP/RSTP, LACP, QoS, ACL, IGMP snooping, port security
comparison.best_for: Enterprise, multi-VLAN, converged network, high reliability

1_managed_vs_unmanaged.recommendation: For dedicated camera network: Unmanaged is sufficient. For shared network with data/voice/video: Managed (EI or SI) required for VLAN separation.

2_100mbps_vs_gigabit.principle: Port speed must support camera bitrate with headroom

comparison.note: For 8MP cameras: 100Mbps is marginal. Use Gigabit.

comparison.uplink_bottleneck: Gigabit uplink supports full switch throughput
comparison.note: Always preferred. Price difference is small.

2_100mbps_vs_gigabit.uplink_sizing: Total camera bandwidth on switch must not exceed uplink port speed. For 16× 4MP H.265 cameras: 16×4=64Mbps → 100Mbps uplink OK. For 16× 8MP H.265: 16×8=128Mbps → Gigabit uplink required.

3_poe_budget_sizing.principle: Total PoE budget must exceed sum of all connected device power consumption

poe_standards.use: Motorized cameras, PTZ 4-inch, cameras with heater
Large PTZ 5/7-inch, cameras with heater + wiper

3_poe_budget_sizing.sizing_formula: Total_power = sum(camera_watts) must be < switch_PoE_budget. Add 20% margin.
3_poe_budget_sizing.example: 8 cameras × 9W each = 72W. DS-3E0510P-E/M has 110W budget → 72/110 = 65% utilization → OK.

4_port_count_sizing.principle: Switch port count must cover all devices plus uplinks and spares
4_port_count_sizing.formula: Required ports = cameras + NVR + uplink(s) + spare (10-20%)

guide.switch: Multiple switches cascaded via uplink ports

5_network_topology.principle: Star topology from switch to cameras, cascade or fiber for multi-switch

topologies.description: One PoE switch → all cameras directly connected
topologies.best_for: Single building, all cameras within 100m of switch

topologies.description: Edge PoE switches → Gigabit uplink to core switch → NVR
topologies.note: Use Gigabit uplinks between switches. Limit cascade to 3 levels.

topologies.description: Edge PoE switches with SFP uplink → fiber → core switch
topologies.note: Use SFP models (DS-3E1510P-EI/M-8P2F) or media converters

6_vlan_design.principle: VLANs isolate camera traffic from data network for security and performance
6_vlan_design.when_needed: When cameras share the same physical network as office computers, VoIP phones, or other devices

typical_design.trunk_ports: Uplink ports configured as trunks carrying all VLANs

6_vlan_design.requires: Managed switch (EI or SI series). Unmanaged switches cannot do VLANs.

7_long_distance_poe.principle: Standard PoE is limited to 100m. Extended features allow longer runs.

options.note: Some Hikvision switches support 250m at 10Mbps per port. Sufficient for 2MP H.265. Enable via DIP switch.
Add inline PoE extender every 100m. Powers from incoming PoE.
Convert copper to fiber. Use SFP-equipped switch or separate converter.
Point-to-point wireless for remote cameras. Add PoE injector at remote end.

8_qos_for_video.principle: QoS prioritizes video traffic to prevent packet loss and jitter
8_qos_for_video.requires: Managed switch (EI or SI). Unmanaged switches have no QoS.
8_qos_for_video.note: For dedicated camera networks: QoS is less critical since all traffic is video. For converged networks: QoS is essential.

9_power_and_installation.principle: Switch requires reliable power, cooling, and physical security

requirements.power_supply: Internal PSU (AC 100-240V). No external adapter for rack models.
requirements.ups: UPS recommended for all PoE switches. If switch loses power, ALL cameras go offline.
requirements.ups_sizing: Switch power consumption + total PoE load. Example: DS-3E0518P-E: 95W switch + 230W PoE = 325W total.
requirements.cooling: Ensure ventilation. Fan-cooled models need airflow clearance. Fanless models for quiet environments.
requirements.rack_mount: 18-port and 26-port models are typically 19-inch rack mountable. 5/8/10-port models are desktop.
requirements.location: Install in lockable network cabinet or comms room. Protect from unauthorized access.

10_compliance.principle: Network switches must meet safety, EMC, and networking standards
